# Questions in Livestream 216

Live Stream #216: Rock Around The Ark Tonight

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:28</a> | What do you make of Kenneth Kitchen explicitly denying northern locations in his Avaris and Raamses discussion? | He says Kitchen rejects Tell el-Daba as Pi-Ramesses but not as Avaris, and instead locates Ramesses farther north near Qantir. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=783" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:03</a> | Why is the river in Joshua 24:2 usually taken as the Euphrates instead of the Jordan? | Because the patriarchal setting points east toward Mesopotamia, so he reads the river there as the Euphrates, not the Jordan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=1048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:28</a> | If I could travel back to the ancient Near East, what would impress me more: vowels and punctuation, or sandwiches and grilled cheese? | He says the food would be more striking, since familiar punctuation is less impressive than discovering ancient flatbread-and-cheese combinations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=1209" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:09</a> | Could the nodules on Amenhotep II have been a postmortem reaction to embalming materials? | He says no; he thinks the marks reflect a pox-like condition, possibly smallpox, that Amenhotep II died from rather than long-survived scars or embalming effects. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=1614" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:54</a> | Is the people determinative on the Merneptah Stele a problem for an early-exodus or judges-period dating of Israel? | No. He says it marks Israel as an ethnos rather than a settled place-name, which still fits an Israel not yet fully urbanized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=1803" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:03</a> | What are your thoughts on the Sabu disc? | He treats it as an odd but probably mundane artifact, not persuasive evidence of lost advanced technology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=1938" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:18</a> | Any thoughts on making a response video to Scott Stripling's Second Exodus Conference lecture? | He has not watched the lecture yet, so he is not ready to respond to it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=2094" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:54</a> | Could the decreasing ages in Genesis reflect nearer ancestors being remembered individually while earlier ones were merged? | He says that is broadly possible within a clan-style reading, since genealogical compression can make nearer figures more individualized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=2391" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:51</a> | Why would clan members stop trying to keep a clan together and instead split into sections inside cities? | Because urban life breaks clans into mixed neighborhoods and new economic ties, so city growth naturally erodes older clan cohesion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=2654" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:14</a> | In Proverbs 7:6-9, is the young man used to these streets and people or not? | He reads the scene as a naive young man drifting into danger rather than someone seasoned in that environment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=2923" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:43</a> | Which biblical retelling of the exodus gets overlooked most? | He points to Moses's birth in Exodus 2, reading the basket among the reeds as an overlooked foreshadowing of Israel's later passage through the reeds. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=3164" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:44</a> | What is your favorite retelling of the exodus narrative? | In Scripture, he says the crucifixion is his favorite retelling because Christ is the Passover lamb; outside Scripture, he still likes The Ten Commandments despite its flaws. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=3366" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:06</a> | How is baptism spiritually efficacious? | He says baptism is a promise: without true commitment it has no efficacy, but if the promise is kept, God keeps the believer in the faith. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=3690" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:30</a> | How is communion spiritually efficacious? | He says communion is spiritually efficacious when it is received in true worship and real fellowship with Christ and other believers, not as mere bread and wine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=3930" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:30</a> | Are baptism and communion simply symbolic? | No. He says both are signs with real covenant force, not mere reminders with no spiritual effect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=4730" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:50</a> | Could you explain the towns in Joshua being described as forts? | He says the Hebrew term can cover anything from an unwalled hamlet to a fortified city, so each place has to be evaluated in context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=4853" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:53</a> | Is the Sermon on the Mount about the kingdom of heaven? | Yes, but he says it is also about Christ's kingdom on earth and, more centrally, about raising the standard so high that it shows people's need for God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=5679" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:39</a> | Did Pharaoh's daughter find Moses in Avaris? | Possibly in that broader delta setting, since he places the Hebrews in that region. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=5764" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:04</a> | Why is Joseph shaved before seeing Pharaoh? | Because Egyptian court etiquette required a shaved appearance, especially in a culture that linked hair with dirt, lice, and lack of refinement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=5828" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:08</a> | Does "shaved" there mean clean-shaven or just trimmed? | He takes it as a full shave, not a light trim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=5917" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:37</a> | How did Egyptians shave? | They used bronze razors, including convex disc-like blades sharpened on leather. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=6045" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:45</a> | Did the Hyksos adopt Egyptian practices too? | Yes. He says they adopted a number of Egyptian customs while still remaining a foreign ruling group. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=6131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:11</a> | I've never seen an Indiana Jones movie. Should I? | He says yes, as long as you treat them as fun adventure movies rather than realistic archaeology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=6576" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:36</a> | What is the most unrealistic thing about archaeology in the Indiana Jones movies? | He says the speed and drama are wildly unrealistic, especially the idea that major finds happen through solo derring-do instead of slow documentation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=7025" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:05</a> | Is Genesis 13:7 an anachronism? | He does not think the verse creates a serious problem and treats such wording as compatible with later scribal updating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=7098" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:18</a> | Are there parallels between Horus's birth and Moses's birth? | He says the parallels are weak and not persuasive as a meaningful source relationship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=7547" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:47</a> | Why is Abimelech called king of the Philistines in Genesis 26:1? | He leans toward retrojection or later labeling rather than taking it as firm proof of full Iron Age Philistines already being there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=7695" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:15</a> | Why do some biblical writers seem to lean on Leviticus while others lean on Deuteronomy? | He says that could reflect preference, local access, or inspiration, but the writers evidently knew both legal traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=7756" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:16</a> | What do you think of Christine Hayes's Old Testament lectures? | He has not watched enough of them to offer a real opinion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=7944" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:24</a> | What are your thoughts on the Christus Victor model of atonement? | He says he does not like it because he sees it as tied to ransom-theory assumptions and, in some uses, vulnerable to low Christology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=8120" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:20</a> | Is Christus Victor different from simply saying Christ is victorious over death and hell? | Yes. He says all orthodox atonement views affirm Christ's victory, while Christus Victor as a model is specifically tied to ransom-theory ideas about Satan holding humanity hostage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=8306" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:26</a> | What denominations most clearly teach Christus Victor? | He associates it most strongly with Eastern Orthodoxy and some related traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=8890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:10</a> | Who needs a pastor if you can have an AI pastor? | He says some people are already using AI for therapy-like or spiritual roles, but he warns that AI tends to flatter and reinforce users rather than tell them hard truths. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=9318" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:18</a> | How much does AI impair academic research going forward? | He says it is seriously damaging research because students who rely on AI for writing do not learn the material or build the thinking habits needed for later scholarship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=9840" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:00</a> | Is there a spell-check program you would recommend over Grammarly? | He says to use a basic spell-checker, like the one in Word, and avoid Grammarly-style tools that start rewriting your style for you. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=9963" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:03</a> | Where can I find the AI paper you mentioned? | He says he had seen it mentioned in a recent video and then looked up and posted the video link in the chat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=10172" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:32</a> | Did ordinary Egyptians remove shoes before their superiors? | No. He says that was not a normal social custom in Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=10292" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:32</a> | Was Isaac basically calm about the sacrifice? | Probably not. He treats Isaac as a real participant in a frightening event, not as someone casually fine with it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=10885" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:25</a> | How should we understand the binding of Isaac? | He says the question is too large to answer adequately in a short livestream response and notes that the passage has generated huge debate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=11026" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:46</a> | Did the people of Avaris practice child sacrifice? | He says there is no evidence that they did. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=11058" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:18</a> | Could Genesis 25:2 support the Midianite hypothesis? | Only in a limited way. He says the verse uses toponymic regional names, so it is not strong evidence by itself, though someone might press it in that direction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=11305" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:25</a> | Why did Jacob send others ahead in groups before meeting Esau? | To soften Esau up and protect himself. He says Jacob was ingratiating himself and effectively putting buffers between Esau and himself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=11705" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:05</a> | Why couldn't Isaac take the blessing back? | Because the blessing was a one-time covenantal appeal before God, so once given to the designated heir it was final. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=11896" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:16</a> | Did Esau really want the blessing rather than the birthright? | Yes. He says the blessing effectively restored the birthright anyway, so Esau could afford to despise the birthright as long as he expected the blessing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=12096" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:36</a> | Is Jacob testing God in Genesis 28:20-22? | Maybe; he says Jacob is making a conditional vow or deal with God, which was common but not a spiritually mature practice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=12451" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:31</a> | Is making this kind of deal with God a good practice? | No. He calls it spiritually immature and says people should not make deals or oaths to God that they may not keep. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=12559" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:19</a> | Can you make a conditional oath to God? | Yes, but he warns that if the condition is met, the oath must be kept. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=12822" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:42</a> | Which Levitical laws applied to all Israel, and was Deuteronomy for all Israel or just Levites? | He says it depends on the specific law: some commands were priestly, while others were for the whole nation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=12867" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:27</a> | When should a believer be baptized? | He prefers baptism soon after a genuine conversion rather than delaying it for a long time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=12980" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:20</a> | How are Hebrew and Egyptian poetry different? | He says they are actually quite similar, since both rely heavily on couplet-like structures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=13670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:50</a> | If Jephthah's daughter was sacrificed, why did she lament her virginity? | He says she was lamenting her lost posterity and legacy, not entering a vestal-virgin role, because Israel did not have such a category. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=14010" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:30</a> | Is animal sacrifice a form of blood magic? | No. He treats it as covenantal sacrificial worship, not magic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=14075" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:35</a> | Could you explain Spirited Away and Totoro? | He reads Spirited Away as dealing with child exploitation and Totoro as a layered story about death in an agricultural community. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=14369" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:29</a> | Is that Totoro interpretation confirmed by the creators? | He says Miyazaki did not state it overtly, but he sees it as suggested by comments about the news story that inspired the film. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=14812" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:06:52</a> | Does the Rumpelstiltskin story have ancient or demonic roots? | He is doubtful and says he does not know of a solid ancient-demonic line behind it, beyond later folklore development. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=14919" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:08:39</a> | Do the "to this day" verses in Joshua help date the book? | A little. He says they can set a rough horizon for some passages, but not enough to settle the dating question by themselves. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=15022" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:10:22</a> | Why did Israel keep taking on the gods of defeated peoples? | He says that reflects the general pull of polytheism: once many gods are assumed to be available, people keep adding more rather than staying exclusive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=15258" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:14:18</a> | Since Torah means law or instruction, how should we understand "law" in the New Testament? | He says Torah really does mean law, and the New Testament understands it through Christ, who fulfills the ceremonial law and is Lord of the Sabbath. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=15421" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:17:01</a> | Did the Old Kingdom end because of a mega-drought, and how could that happen if Egypt lived off the Nile? | He says drought played a role, but the real collapse came from political decentralization, nomarch power, and civil war. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=15595" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:19:55</a> | Why do we only see the sandal-removal command here and in Exodus 3? | He says it marks sacred space, but especially because leather sandals were animal products and thus ritually unclean for temple-like holy ground. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=15772" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:22:52</a> | Did Moses think he was meant to be a deliverer when he killed the Egyptian, or did he just lash out in anger? | He says it was an act of anger and overreaction, not a justified deliverer move. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=15900" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:25:00</a> | How does Genesis point to the exodus, and how do the rest of the Old and New Testaments point back to it? | He says Genesis lays ritual and covenant foundations that culminate in Exodus, and later Scripture keeps reusing the exodus as the central redemption pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=15941" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:25:41</a> | If plastic is made from dead dinosaurs, is it ritually unclean like leather? | He says petroleum-derived materials are treated as ritually clean because the material has been processed and purified by the earth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=16107" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:28:27</a> | Were any parts of the pre-exilic books written from Babylon, and how would we know? | He says you would look for signals such as Persian or other exile-era loanwords, which can point to a Babylonian setting or later composition horizon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=16201" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:30:01</a> | Is Rahab's story a Passover reference? | Yes, he sees a real Passover echo in the rescue logic and the marked household. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=16460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:34:20</a> | Why frogs in the plagues? What is so bad about frogs? | He says frogs were a water-supply problem: dead or toxic frogs in wells, houses, and stored drink could poison what people depended on. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=16650" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:37:30</a> | Does the name Yahweh itself imply an unmoved mover? | He does not think the divine name by itself proves that philosophical idea. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=16715" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:38:35</a> | Is Genesis 1 a polemic against polytheism? | Yes, in part. He likes reading it as a demythologizing text that strips rival divine powers of real godhood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=16901" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:41:41</a> | How do you think Sarah handled the binding of Isaac afterward? | He assumes she would have been deeply upset rather than casually at peace with it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=17063" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:44:23</a> | Is the Muslim God the same as the Christian God? | No. He says the attributes, gospel, and Christology are different, so shared name-language is not enough to make them the same God or Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=18144" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:02:24</a> | Have you heard people refer to the Holy Spirit as "the Lord"? | Yes. He says it is not incorrect, since the Holy Spirit is called God in the New Testament and "Lord" translates Yahweh. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=18244" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:04:04</a> | Who commissioned Vatican II? | He thinks it was probably Pope John XXIII, but says he could be wrong and treats the answer as uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/5bDfwDUvOiQ?t=18311" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:05:11</a> | How do you keep your goatee so even? | He says he combs it and trims it with scissors, though sometimes it ends up shorter than other times. |
